sources_checked.limitation: Initial mcp__workspace__web_fetch returned only nav/footer shell (menu items not present in static fetch, likely client-rendered). Escalated to Claude-in-Chrome: navigated to the same URL, used read_page/get_page_text to extract the fully rendered 'Dinner Menu' section, which includes the restaurant's own bracketed allergen tags (Dairy, Gluten, Tree Nut, Peanuts, Egg, Shellfish, Sesame, Low-Gluten, Vegan, Vegetarian). A 'Drinks Menu' tab/section also exists on the page but its content/URL was not located within the interactive-element listing during the time-boxed session, so beverages are NOT included in this extraction.

identity_verification_notes: Site footer confirms '24 First Avenue, New York, NY 10009', matching target address exactly. Restaurant is the contemporary Indian restaurant co-founded by chef Vikas Khanna, opened 2024, in NYC East Village -- distinguished from unrelated same-named 'Bungalow' venues elsewhere (e.g. other cities/beach clubs) which appeared in search results and were not used.
manual_review_reason: Drinks/beverage menu not captured (not found within time box); food menu is complete and current with restaurant-declared allergen tags for gluten, peanut, and tree nut. Also note: 'Farmers Sattu Roti' is labeled by the restaurant as 'Low-Gluten' (not gluten-free) -- treated here as a declared-gluten item since it explicitly indicates gluten presence, but a human reviewer may want to double check this labeling nuance. Coconut-containing dishes were flagged as low-confidence 'likely' tree nut per FDA allergen-labeling convention, which some reviewers may consider too conservative.
